role: This file is the visible walkthrough/review card region rendered during an active session, presenting either a guided tour or a step-by-step review UI and wiring operator verdicts back through AppContext.
capabilities: Show session progress and close/end the walk
Guide mode: narrate the addressed UI element, re-spotlight it, step through the tour, and show completion
Review mode: display item framing and associated UI target
Capture a verdict reason via text input
Submit operator verdicts: approve, reject, comment, skip, decide
Disable respond/next controls while a request is in flight (wtBusy guard)
substance_why: It implements the full conditional UI for both guided tours and review walkthroughs, including wired controls, concurrency guards, and context-driven state.
interactions_present: Approve, reject, comment, skip, decide verdict buttons
interactions_missing: No retry or error-recovery UI for failed requests
No keyboard shortcuts for verdicts or navigation
No confirmation before closing the walkthrough
No cancellation UI for long in-flight requests
No loading/progress indication beyond disabled state and busy text
performance_risks: Reason input onChange updates context state and may re-render the whole app on every keystroke
Inline arrow functions are recreated each render, potentially causing unnecessary child re-renders
Long-running requests (~20s) block the card without cancellation, timeout, or progress UI
No memoization of the guide/review branches or derived values
reuse_why: The domain concepts (guided tour, review verdicts, busy guards, voice toggle) are correct, but the implementation mixes both modes and is tightly coupled to AppContext, so it should be rebuilt as composable sub-components.
decomposition_hints: Split GuideWalkthrough and ReviewWalkthrough into separate components
Extract a shared WalkthroughShell for head, progress, voice toggle, close, and footer
Extract a VerdictBar component that owns reason input and verdict buttons (review only)
Extract a SpotlightButton / resolveUiTarget trigger reused by both modes
Move busy-guard logic and status display into a shared action guard or footer component
